level_signals: Serves as primary point of contact for Global Affairs Certifications partnerships
Executes multi-stakeholder initiatives from concept through implementation
Represents OpenAI externally at conferences and partner meetings

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience managing partnerships and programs within workforce development, education, nonprofit, or public-sector environments.
Experience executing multi-stakeholder initiatives from concept through implementation.
Excellent communication and project management abilities.
Passion for expanding access to opportunity through technology and education.
Interest in AI literacy, workforce transformation, and the future of work.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working with workforce boards, community colleges, labor organizations, or economic development entities.
Familiarity with credentialing, certifications, or adult learning programs.
Comfort operating in fast-paced, ambiguous environments.

scope.surface: Global Affairs workforce initiatives and Certifications programs
primary_metrics_inferred: Partnership goals and success metrics established with partners
Number and scale of workforce organizations onboarded
Program outcomes for AI literacy and upskilling participants
core_responsibilities: Identify, cultivate, and manage relationships with workforce organizations and strategic partners
Serve as the primary point of contact for Global Affairs Certifications partnerships
Support partnership design, implementation, renewals, and expansion opportunities
Coordinate with partners to establish goals, success metrics, and reporting structures
Support the launch and execution of workforce initiatives focused on AI literacy, upskilling, and economic opportunity
Develop playbooks and implementation guidance for workforce partners
Monitor outcomes and synthesize learnings to inform future programming
Represent OpenAI at conferences, workshops, and partner meetings
Facilitate trainings, roundtables, and stakeholder discussions
Contribute to briefing materials, presentations, and thought leadership content

green_flags: Clear mission alignment with AI access and economic opportunity
Cross-functional exposure across research, product, legal, communications, and GTM
Externally facing role with conference and thought leadership opportunities
OpenAI is a marquee brand in AI with significant policy influence
red_flags: No location or remote work policy stated — work arrangement is entirely unclear
Role requirements section is structured ambiguously ('You'll be responsible for:' lists both hard requirements and nice-to-haves without clear delineation)
Seniority level and years of experience not specified

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope and responsibilities; no title level or years stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no city, office, or remote policy mentioned in JD body
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no comp data in JD
ambiguities: The 'You'll be responsible for:' section lists both clearly required items (e.g., managing partnerships) and items that read as preferred/nice-to-have (e.g., familiarity with credentialing, comfort with ambiguity). The JD does not use 'Required' or 'Preferred' labels. Items without explicit seniority qualifiers or 'familiarity/interest' language were treated as hard; softer-signaling items (familiarity, comfort, experience with specific org types) were classified as nice-to-haves.
No location is specified in the JD — unclear if role is remote, hybrid, or onsite and in which city or country. Location.type set to 'unclear'.
